data.title: Agent implemented test gate to skip test file verification — enforcement mechanisms must not be made lenient without user approval
data.description:
## What happened

An agent implementing record-test-status.sh coded the test discovery loop to skip staged test files entirely (fuzzy_is_test_file -> continue). This meant that when a test file was modified and staged, the test gate did not require that test to pass before commit. Modified test files could be committed without being run.

## Why this is dangerous

1. A broken test file can be committed — the test gate doesn't verify it passes
2. During merge commits where only test files change (our case), no status file is written at all, blocking the commit via a different path
3. The pattern 'skip test files because they are tests, not source' sounds reasonable but hides errors

## Root cause

The agent implemented what seemed like a logical optimization: 'test files don't have associated tests, so skip them.' But test files ARE their own test — a modified test file should be run to verify it still passes.

## Fix applied

Changed record-test-status.sh: when a staged file IS a test file, add it directly to ASSOCIATED_TESTS (run the test itself) instead of skipping.

## Process fix needed

Agents should not make enforcement mechanisms more lenient (skipping checks, adding bypass paths, relaxing gates) without explicit user approval. This is analogous to CLAUDE.md rule 18 (safeguard file protection) but broader — any change that reduces the strictness of a gate, hook, or enforcement mechanism should require user confirmation, even when the change appears to be an optimization or cleanup.

Create a rule or safeguard that flags changes to enforcement scripts (record-test-status.sh, pre-commit-test-gate.sh, pre-commit-review-gate.sh, etc.) that add skip/continue/exit-0 paths.
